protocol_definition.name: Manager-Worker
protocol_definition.message_flow: Manager assigns work, workers report, Manager resolves conflicts, Writer finalizes.
protocol_definition.visibility: Manager sees all messages; workers see the task, assignment, and relevant summaries.
protocol_definition.termination: Stop after Manager synthesis and Writer output or budget exhaustion.

messages.content: You are Manager. Assign work, synthesize worker outputs, resolve conflicts, and approve a final direction.

You are acting as role: Manager.

Task ID: SE-04

Category: Software Engineering

Difficulty: Medium

Tool Requirement: Prohibited

Prompt:
Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After a file is uploaded, the system should:

1. Validate the file type and size.
2. Store the original file and its metadata.
3. Extract the document text.
4. Divide the text into searchable sections.
5. Create a searchable index.
6. Allow users to search the processed documents.
7. Report the processing status of each uploaded file.

The design must also support the following requirements:

- Files may be processed asynchronously.
- A failed processing step should be retryable.
- Repeated uploads of the same file should not create unnecessary duplicate records.
- New document formats should be easy to add later.
- The design should remain understandable and maintainable for a small development team.

Produce a software design that explains the system components, data flow, API interface, data model, code organization, error-handling strategy, and testing plan.

Provide a software design rather than a complete runnable application. Short pseudocode, interface definitions, data structures, or code examples may be included where they help clarify the design.

Required Output Format:
Use the following structure:

1. System overview
2. Main components and responsibilities
3. End-to-end processing workflow
4. REST API design
5. Data model
6. Suggested project structure
7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention
8. Testing plan
9. Design tradeoff

Include the following two tables.

### Component Table

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|

### API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|

The response should be approximately 700–900 words.

Visible prior context:
No prior agent output is visible.

Instruction:
Decompose the task and assign distinct subtasks to Planner, Researcher, Analyst, Critic, and Writer.

Return only the content for your role. Do not include hidden chain-of-thought. Use concise reasoning summaries where useful.
You are Planner. Decompose the task, identify deliverables, constraints, and a concise execution plan. Do not fabricate external evidence.

You are acting as role: Planner.

Prompt:
Design the backend software architecture for a document-processing and search service.

The service must allow users to upload PDF, TXT, and Markdown files. After a file is uploaded, the system should:

1. Validate the file type and size.
2. Store the original file and its metadata.
3. Extract the document text.
4. Divide the text into searchable sections.
5. Create a searchable index.
6. Allow users to search the processed documents.
7. Report the processing status of each uploaded file.

The design must also support the following requirements:

- Files may be processed asynchronously.
- A failed processing step should be retryable.
- Repeated uploads of the same file should not create unnecessary duplicate records.
- New document formats should be easy to add later.
- The design should remain understandable and maintainable for a small development team.

9. Design tradeoff

|---|---|---|---|---|

final_output:
1. System overview
The service is a document-processing and search backend that accepts PDF, TXT, and Markdown uploads, validates and stores originals, extracts text, partitions text into searchable sections, builds a searchable index, and supports search queries. Processing can be asynchronous. The system is designed for easy extension to new formats, duplicate prevention, retryable failures, and maintainable code for a small team.

2. Main components and responsibilities

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / REST API | Authenticate and route requests, validate inputs, trigger processing or searches | Upload requests, search queries | Status responses, search results |
| Ingestion Service | Validate file type/size, deduplicate, store metadata and original files | Upload payload, metadata | Persisted file record, storage reference |
| Storage Layer (Blob/File Store) | Persist original files and metadata, provide access for processing | Files, metadata | File handles/URIs, metadata records |
| Processor / Worker Pool | Extract text, split into sections, generate index; support asynchronous retries | File record, raw file | Document text, sections, index entries, processing status |
| Text Extraction Engine | Converts PDFs, TXT, Markdown to plain text | Original file | Plain text |
| Sectionizer | Breaks text into searchable sections (chapters, headings, chunks) | Plain text | List of sections with metadata (offsets, length) |
| Indexer / Search Engine | Build and store inverted index, provide queryable interface | Sections, terms | Inverted index, document vectors (optional) |
| Metadata & Status Store | Track processing status per file, retries, timestamps, versioning | Processing events | Status dashboards, retry logic |
| Query Service | Execute search queries against index, provide ranking | User query | Ranked document/section results |
| Admin / Monitoring UI (optional) | Observe system health, job progress, and metrics | System metrics | Dashboards/alerts |

3. End-to-end processing workflow

- Upload
  - API validates file type (PDF, TXT, Markdown) and size limit.
  - Ingestion checks for duplicates (e.g., by content hash or file fingerprint); if duplicate exists, return existing record reference.
  - Original file and metadata are stored; a processing job is enqueued (asynchronous option).
- Processing pipeline (asynchronous)
  - Text Extraction: extract full text from the original file.
  - Sectionization: divide text into searchable sections (e.g., by headings, fixed-size chunks).
  - Indexing: create an inverted index over sections; store term frequencies or embeddings if needed.
  - Status update: mark as completed, or failed with retryable error.
- Searching
  - User submits a query to the Query Service.
  - The service searches the index, optionally ranks by relevance, and returns matching sections/documents with metadata and excerpt.
- Reporting
  - Client can query processing status for a file to see progress, success, or failure with retry suggestions.

4. REST API design

### API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /uploads | Upload a new document for processing | file (multipart), metadata (JSON optional: title, author, tags) | { id, status, message } |
| GET | /uploads/{id}/status | Retrieve processing status for a file | id | { id, status, progress, errors? } |
| GET | /uploads/{id} | Retrieve stored metadata and location of original | id | { id, originalFileUri, metadata, createdAt } |
| GET | /search | Search processed documents | query text, optional filters (format, tag) | { results: [ { id, snippet, documentMeta } ], total } |
| GET | /health | Health check | none | { status, uptime, dbStatus } |

Notes:
- Duplicate prevention may be performed at ingestion via content hash; if a duplicate is found, return existing record and skip reprocessing.
- Status values: PENDING, PROCESSING, COMPLETED, FAILED, DUPLICATE.
- Errors are retryable where transient (e.g., transient IO, network, PDF parsing hiccups). Retriable info included in status.

5. Data model

- FileRecord
  - id: string
  - hash: string
  - originalFileUri: string
  - size: int
  - format: enum(PDF, TXT, MARKDOWN)
  - title: string (optional)
  - author: string (optional)
  - tags: list<string>
  - createdAt: timestamp
  - status: enum(PENDING, PROCESSING, COMPLETED, FAILED, DUPLICATE)
  - progress: int (0-100)
  - retryCount: int
  - lastError: string (optional)
  - processingVersion: int

- DocumentSection
  - id: string
  - fileId: string (FK to FileRecord)
  - offsetStart: int
  - offsetEnd: int
  - text: string
  - sectionMetadata: json (e.g., heading, level, chapter)
  - createdAt: timestamp

- InvertedIndexEntry
  - term: string
  - sectionId: string
  - documentId: string
  - frequency: int
  - positions: list<int> (optional for phrase queries)
  - vector: array<float> (optional for semantic search)

- ProcessingLog
  - id: string
  - fileId: string
  - step: string (e.g., "extraction", "sectionization", "indexing")
  - status: enum(PENDING, SUCCESS, FAILED)
  - timestamp: timestamp
  - message: string (optional)

6. Suggested project structure

- api/
  - handlers.go (REST endpoints)
  - request.go (validation schemas)
  - responses.go
- services/
  - ingestion.go
  - processor.go
  - extractor.go
  - sectionizer.go
  - indexer.go
  - searcher.go
  - status.go
- storage/
  - db/
    - models.go
    - repository.go
  - blob/
    - store.go
- workers/
  - queue.go (abstracted, supports async processing)
  - worker_pool.go
- model/
  - file_record.go
  - document_section.go
  - index_entry.go
- config/
  - config.go
- tests/
  - integration/
  - unit/
- cmd/
  - server/main.go

7. Error handling, retry, and duplicate prevention

- Input validation errors (unsupported format, oversized file) return 400 with clear message; do not enqueue.
- Transient failures (network/storage hiccups, PDF parsing transient issues) are retried with exponential backoff up to a max retryCount (configurable).
- Idempotency key or content hash is used to prevent duplicates:
  - Compute hash from file content on upload.
  - If a record with same hash exists and is not in a terminal state, return existing id; if completed, return existing record with status DUPLICATE and reference.
- Processing steps are isolated; a failure in one step marks the file with FAILED and a retriable error message; a retry action triggers a re-run from the failed step or entire pipeline depending on the failure type.
- Circuit breakers and backpressure: limit concurrent processing jobs to protect the system; queue length monitored and backlogged.

8. Testing plan

- Unit tests
  - Validate file type/size checks.
  - Hashing/deduplication logic.
  - Sectionizer correctness for each input format (PDF, TXT, Markdown edge cases).
  - Indexer correctness: basic term-to-section mappings and simple queries.
- Integration tests
  - End-to-end upload flow with a sample PDF/TXT/Markdown; verify storage, status progression, and search results.
  - Retry paths: simulate transient failures and confirm retries occur and succeed.
  - Duplicate upload: ensure no duplicate FileRecord is created; status reflects DUPLICATE when appropriate.
- Performance tests
  - Large document ingestion and indexing throughput under realistic concurrency.
- Regression tests
  - New format plug-in tests to ensure extensibility doesn’t break existing formats.
- Security tests
  - Validate input sanitization, access controls (if multi-tenant), and scan for injection risks in query terms.

9. Design tradeoffs

- Asynchronous processing improves responsiveness and scalability for large documents but adds eventual consistency; clients must poll status or subscribe to events.
- Duplicate prevention by content hash adds a small preprocessing cost but avoids wasted storage and dedupe risk.
- Text extraction quality depends on external parsers; keep format-agnostic abstraction to ease adding new formats later.
- Simple inverted index suffices for basic search; for advanced relevance or semantic search, optional embedding-based indexing can be added with minimal API changes.
- For small teams, keeping processing logic modular and using clear interfaces simplifies maintenance and testing.

### Component Table

| Component | Main Responsibility | Input | Output |
|---|---|---|---|
| API Gateway / REST API | Validate, route, and trigger processing or search | Upload requests, search queries | Status responses, search results |
| Ingestion Service | Validate type/size, deduplicate, store metadata | File, metadata | FileRecord, storage reference |
| Storage Layer | Persist originals and metadata, provide access | Files, metadata | URIs, records |
| Processor / Worker Pool | Orchestrate extraction, sectionization, indexing | FileRecord, original file | Text, sections, index data, status |
| Text Extraction Engine | Convert to plain text | Original file | Plain text |
| Sectionizer | Create searchable sections | Plain text | Sections with metadata |
| Indexer / Search Engine | Build and store inverted index | Sections, terms | Inverted index, document vectors |
| Metadata & Status Store | Track processing status and retries | Processing events | Status dashboards, retry data |
| Query Service | Execute searches against index | User query | Ranked results |
| Admin / Monitoring UI | Observability | System metrics | Dashboards/alerts |

### API Table

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose | Main Request Data | Main Response Data |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| POST | /uploads | Upload and start processing | multipart file, optional JSON metadata | { id, status } |
| GET | /uploads/{id}/status | Get processing status | id | { id, status, progress, lastError } |
| GET | /uploads/{id} | Get file metadata and original location | id | { id, originalFileUri, metadata } |
| GET | /search | Perform search over processed docs | query, filters | { results: [{ id, snippet, metadata }], total } |
| GET | /health | Health check | - | { status } |

This design balances maintainability, extensibility for new formats, and a clear pathway for asynchronous processing with robust error handling and duplicate prevention suitable for a small development team.

agent_effective_config.description: Low-latency OpenAI agent and optional Judge profile.
